chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with postoperative cardiogenic shock after aortic root replacement.
history: The patient underwent aortic root replacement using an On-X mechanical valve. Due to postoperative cardiogenic shock, an extracorporeal left ventricular assist device (LVAD) was implanted as a bridge to durable LVAD therapy. The patient had a history of valvular disease and was at risk for thromboembolism.
exam: Vital signs: BP 80/50 mmHg, HR 120 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed a patient in cardiogenic shock with decreased peripheral perfusion.
labs: ECG: New Q-waves in leads II, III, and aVF. Bloods: Elevated troponin levels. Imaging: Echocardiogram showed decreased left ventricular function.
differentials: Thromboembolism due to On-X mechanical valve
correct_ranking: Thromboembolism due to On-X mechanical valve
